title: Community Retraining and Opportunities Initiative - Funding Program - Prepare to apply - Canada.ca
content:
The process includes 2 phases:
- Expression of interest : Interested organizations must indicate A mass layoff has occurred that meets the definition for funding eligibility The mass layoff will have a significant impact on the community Local labour market conditions make it difficult for affected works to transition to new jobs
- A mass layoff has occurred that meets the definition for funding eligibility
- The mass layoff will have a significant impact on the community
- Local labour market conditions make it difficult for affected works to transition to new jobs
- Application : Full application is assessed against all requirements.

## Prepare to Submit an Expression of Interest
The following information is required to assess your expression of interest and must all be included in your submission.
- The exact date of the mass layoff(s) (YYYY-MM-DD). If layoffs occurred on multiple days, please include each specific date.
- The name of the community and postal code of the workplace experiencing the mass layoff.
- The number of individuals laid off. If your request is for multiple dates, list the number of individuals by date.
- The 3-digit North American Industry Classification System (NAICS) code or sub-industry for the business or employer where the layoff occurred. Please refer to Statistics Canada for guidance.
- The name of the employer undergoing the layoff(s)
- Source of information regarding the layoff and the number of employees affected. This includes but is not limited to: news reports, corporate messaging, union announcements, letter from community organization.
- Evidence to demonstrate local labour market conditions make it difficult for affected workers to transition to new jobs.
- For expressions of interest with multiple layoffs: A rationale for why the layoffs should be addressed as one project is required.

### Give feedback about this page
Thank you for your feedback.
